canonical_params.expression: volume = ask_size + bid_size; mid_price = (ask_price + bid_price) / 2; liquidity_imbalance = (bid_size - ask_size) / (bid_size + ask_size); matched_imbalance = (imbalance_size - matched_size) / (imbalance_size + matched_size); size_imbalance = bid_size / ask_size

actions.reasoning: Create first-order order-book imbalance features from quoted prices and sizes to summarize spread, depth, and buy/sell pressure at each timestamp.
actions.context_notes: This is the core Optiver feature block and recurs across many LightGBM/XGBoost baselines. `matched_imbalance` is sometimes written as a split eval expression but resolves to the same ratio.

actions.reasoning: Drops far_price and near_price because they have approximately 50% missing values, but these are the core indicative auction price signals essential for microstructure feature engineering.

actions.reasoning: Compute grouped percent-change features over short windows for the most important imbalance and reference-price signals.
actions.context_notes: The schema does not have a dedicated percent-change action, so this recurring pattern is encoded as `APPLY_EXPRESSION`. It normally appears alongside the shift features above.

canonical_params.expression: for each pair of price columns in [reference_price, far_price, near_price, ask_price, bid_price, wap], compute (<price_a> - <price_b>) / <price_b>

actions.reasoning: Uses an asymmetric single-column denominator, making the pairwise imbalance features unstable and scale-dependent instead of normalized between the two prices.

actions.reasoning: Build stock-level quote summary features from bid/ask price and size distributions and join them back to each row as self-contained stock priors.
actions.context_notes: This row stands on its own as a stock-prior strategy: compute per-stock median/std/min/max quote summaries and merge them back by stock_id. Some notebooks stop at these joined summary features, while others continue to collapse them into composite priors.

canonical_params.columns: remaining numeric feature columns after fixed far_price/near_price defaults

actions.reasoning: Impute the remaining missing numeric values with medians conditioned on stock identity and imbalance direction.
actions.context_notes: Alternative (OR) to time-bucket median imputation. This version appears in the Torch DNN pipeline after the larger feature block has already been generated. The grouped median step is only coherent after the Optiver-specific far_price=0 and near_price=1 defaults are already in place.

actions.reasoning: Aggressively clips all price columns at the 1st/99th percentiles, destroying informative auction-tail price signals that are critical for predicting closing price movements.

canonical_params.expression: compute row-wise summary statistics over prices = [reference_price, far_price, near_price, ask_price, bid_price, wap] and sizes = [matched_size, bid_size, ask_size, imbalance_size] using min, max, median, and range

actions.reasoning: Replaces the first-four-moment summary block with extrema-only summaries, discarding the skew and kurtosis cues that capture auction-shape asymmetry.

canonical_params.expression: imbalance_ratio = imbalance_size / (matched_size + ask_size + bid_size)

actions.reasoning: Dilutes the imbalance ratio with both book-side depth columns, turning a direct unmatched-pressure measure into a heavily shrunk volume fraction.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic near-miss of CA-000065: changes the denominator so the ratio no longer isolates unmatched auction pressure versus matched size.

actions.reasoning: Standardize engineered numeric features for linear-model and neural-network pipelines that are sensitive to feature scale.
actions.context_notes: Rare and model-specific. Tree-based Optiver pipelines usually skip explicit scaling, but the linear and transformer notebooks do not.

actions.reasoning: Fill `far_price` and `near_price` with their respective global column means before building transformer-style sequence features.

canonical_params.expression: within each date_id ordered by [stock_id, seconds_in_bucket], compute pct_change(window) for matched_size, imbalance_size, reference_price, and imbalance_buy_sell_flag at windows [1, 2, 3, 10]

actions.reasoning: Fill missing `near_price` with one before additional preprocessing.
actions.context_notes: The fill value is consistently `1` rather than `0`, so it needs its own canonical action instead of being merged with the `far_price` default.

canonical_params.expression: volume = abs(ask_size - bid_size); mid_price = ask_price + bid_price; liquidity_imbalance = (bid_size + ask_size) / max(abs(bid_size - ask_size), 1); matched_imbalance = imbalance_size / max(matched_size, 1); size_imbalance = ask_size / max(bid_size, 1)

canonical_params.expression: fill far_price and near_price with 0, then compute row-wise summary statistics over prices = [reference_price, far_price, near_price, ask_price, bid_price, wap] and sizes = [matched_size, bid_size, ask_size, imbalance_size] using mean, std, skew, and kurtosis

actions.reasoning: Compute stock-level rolling standard deviations over the recent six time steps for the main price columns to capture local price volatility.

actions.context_notes: Optiver feature sets expand quickly after imbalance, lag, and rolling features. Exact numpy width varies by notebook, but the canonical action is numeric float downcasting for memory efficiency.

actions.reasoning: Applies standard scaling to raw price columns before lag/diff temporal features are computed, distorting the time-series structure and making temporal differences meaningless.

The grouped median step is only coherent after the Optiver-specific far_price=0 and near_price=1 defaults are already in place.

canonical_params.expression: imbalance_momentum = diff(imbalance_size, periods=1) within stock_id ordered by [date_id, seconds_in_bucket] / matched_size; price_spread = ask_price - bid_price; spread_intensity = diff(price_spread, periods=1) within stock_id ordered by [date_id, seconds_in_bucket]; price_pressure = imbalance_size * (ask_price - bid_price); market_urgency = price_spread * liquidity_imbalance; depth_pressure = (ask_size - bid_size) * (far_price - near_price)

actions.reasoning: Add second-order microstructure interaction features that combine imbalance, spread, and depth into stronger short-horizon pressure signals.
actions.context_notes: Several notebooks lift this feature block nearly verbatim. `spread_intensity` and `imbalance_momentum` both rely on stock-level time ordering, which the canonical expression spells out explicitly.

canonical_params.expression: imbalance_ratio = abs(imbalance_size - matched_size) / max(matched_size, 1)

actions.reasoning: Collapses the signed imbalance relationship into an absolute gap, removing whether the book is tilted toward buy or sell pressure.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic near-miss of CA-000065: keeps the scale but discards the sign of the imbalance relationship by taking an absolute gap.

canonical_params.expression: imbalance_ratio = imbalance_size / matched_size

actions.reasoning: Compute the ratio of auction imbalance size to matched size as a direct measure of unmatched order pressure.
actions.context_notes: This simpler ratio feature appears across a wide swath of baseline LightGBM/XGBoost notebooks and complements the symmetric `matched_imbalance` ratio already captured elsewhere. Some notebooks later replace infinite values produced by zero denominators.

actions.reasoning: Drops near_price by itself because it is roughly half missing, but near_price is one of the core indicative auction-price signals and is especially important for spread- and depth-style expressions.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Extended the high-null-family coverage with a narrower Optiver-specific variant that drops only one of the two indicative auction price columns.

actions.reasoning: Aggressively clips the size columns at the 5th/95th percentiles and erases the large depth spikes that are often the whole point of the Optiver auction state.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Adds a second explicit clipping fault by over-winsorizing the auction depth columns rather than the price columns.

actions.reasoning: Keeps only buy-pressure rows and throws away the sell-pressure half of the auction history, creating a strongly biased training distribution.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Adds a row-filtering distractor that conditions the training set on one imbalance direction instead of modeling both sides of the order book.

actions.reasoning: Drops the first two-thirds of the closing-auction sequence and leaves the model to learn only from the final minute, discarding most temporal context.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Adds a time-based row filter that mistakes late-auction concentration for a legitimate preprocessing step.

actions.reasoning: Creates missing-value flags for identifier and sign columns that do not have genuine missingness, producing constant-zero features with no information content.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Broadens missingness distractors with an Optiver-specific variant that adds indicators to fully observed ID-style columns.

actions.reasoning: Creates missing indicators for the fully observed core quote-price columns, adding dead-weight features that only increase dimensionality.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Adds a second missing-indicator distractor for fully observed price columns that never need null flags.

actions.reasoning: Drops every row where either indicative auction price is missing, which removes a large fraction of the train set and guarantees the model never learns from the missingness pattern itself.
